title: Freezer Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The freezer compartment temperature sensor is not providing valid readings.
fullDescription: Error E01 indicates the freezer temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) has failed or its circuit is open or shorted. The main control board cannot read the freezer temperature and cannot regulate the compressor and defrost cycles properly.

causes.cause: Freezer NTC thermistor has failed
Sensor connector is loose or corroded
Sensor wiring is damaged from ice buildup
Main control board sensor input has failed

symptoms: Freezer temperature is too warm or too cold
Compressor runs continuously or not at all
Temperature display shows error or dashes

fixSteps.title: Locate the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The freezer sensor is typically clipped to the evaporator cover or mounted on the wall inside the freezer compartment. Consult your model's tech sheet for exact location.

fixSteps.title: Check the connector
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Access the sensor connector and ensure it is firmly seated. Clean any frost or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At 32F/0C it should read approximately 32,000 ohms. At room temperature approximately 12,000 ohms. OL or near zero indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Unclip or unscrew the old sensor, disconnect the wiring, and install the replacement in the same position.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear E01, the main board needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E01 freezer sensor error fix

faq.q: Will E01 cause food to spoil?
faq.a: If the freezer temperature is not regulated, food may thaw. Move perishables to another freezer if the error persists.

faq.q: Is the Bosch fridge sensor the same as Siemens?
faq.a: Bosch and Siemens share many refrigerator platforms and parts. The sensor may be the same part number, but always verify.

faq.q: Can frost cause E01?
faq.a: Yes. Excessive frost buildup can damage sensor wiring or connectors. Defrosting the freezer may temporarily resolve the issue.

title: Refrigerator Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The fresh food compartment temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the fresh food (refrigerator) compartment temperature sensor has an open or shorted circuit. The control board cannot properly regulate the damper or cooling for the fresh food section.

causes.cause: Refrigerator NTC thermistor has failed
Sensor connector is disconnected or corroded
Wiring damaged by ice or physical damage
Control board sensor input failure

symptoms: Refrigerator section too warm or too cold
Fresh food freezing when it should not

fixSteps.title: Locate the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The fresh food sensor is usually mounted behind the rear panel inside the refrigerator compartment, near the air inlet from the freezer.

fixSteps.title: Check the connector
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Remove the sensor cover panel and check the connector for tightness and corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At 37F/3C it should read approximately 27,000 ohms. At room temperature approximately 12,000 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor from its mount and install the new one. Reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the new sensor does not resolve E02, the main board or damper system may need professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E02 fridge sensor error fix

faq.q: Can E02 cause food to freeze in the fridge?
faq.a: Yes. Without accurate temperature reading, the control may overcool the fresh food section, causing items to freeze.

faq.q: Is the fridge sensor the same part as the freezer sensor?
faq.a: On many Bosch models they are the same part number. Verify for your specific model.

faq.q: Should I keep the fridge running with E02?
faq.a: The fridge will continue to run but temperature regulation may be poor. Monitor food temperatures and address E02 promptly.

title: Evaporator Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The evaporator temperature sensor has failed or is out of range.
fullDescription: Error E03 indicates the evaporator (defrost) temperature sensor has an open or shorted circuit. This sensor monitors the evaporator coil temperature to control defrost timing. Without it, the defrost system cannot operate correctly, leading to frost buildup.

causes.cause: Evaporator sensor has failed
Ice has encased the sensor or damaged its wiring
Sensor connector has come loose during defrost cycling
Main board defrost circuit failure

symptoms: Heavy frost buildup on the evaporator
Ice forming on back wall of freezer

fixSteps.title: Defrost the freezer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator and leave the freezer door open for 24 hours to fully defrost. Excessive ice may be encasing the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Access the evaporator
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the freezer back panel to access the evaporator coil. The sensor is clipped directly to the evaporator tubing.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and measure resistance. At 32F it should read approximately 32,000 ohms. At room temperature approximately 12,000 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Unclip the old sensor from the evaporator, disconnect wiring, and clip the new sensor in place.

whenToCallPro: If the evaporator shows signs of damage or refrigerant leak, call a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E03 evaporator sensor defrost error fix

faq.q: Why does frost cause sensor failure?
faq.a: Ice expanding around the sensor can crack the housing or pull wires loose. Repeated freeze/thaw cycles stress the connections.

faq.q: How long does a full defrost take?
faq.a: Allow 24 hours with the doors open and the refrigerator unplugged for a complete manual defrost.

faq.q: Can I use a hair dryer to speed defrosting?
faq.a: You can use a hair dryer on low heat, but keep it away from plastic parts and wiring to avoid melting or damage.

title: Fan Motor Fault
shortDescription: The evaporator or condenser fan motor is not operating.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates one of the fan motors (evaporator fan or condenser fan) is not running or is running outside its expected speed range. The control board monitors fan motor feedback and triggers E04 when the expected rotation is not detected.

causes.cause: Evaporator fan motor has failed
Fan blade is blocked by ice or debris
Condenser fan motor has failed
Fan motor wiring is damaged

symptoms: No air circulation noise inside the unit
Frost buildup on evaporator (evaporator fan failure)

fixSteps.title: Check for ice blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Open the freezer and listen for the evaporator fan. If you hear a grinding noise or nothing, ice may be blocking the fan. Defrost the unit.

fixSteps.title: Test the evaporator fan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the freezer back panel to access the evaporator fan. Check that the blade spins freely. Test motor windings for resistance.

fixSteps.title: Check the condenser fan
fixSteps.instruction: Access the condenser fan at the bottom rear of the refrigerator. Ensure the blade spins freely and the motor is operational.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the mounting hardware for the failed fan motor, disconnect wiring, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If both fans test fine, the control board may not be sending power to the motors, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E04 fan motor error fix

faq.q: Which fan is more likely to fail?
faq.a: The evaporator fan fails more often because it operates in freezing conditions and is more susceptible to ice-related damage.

faq.q: Can I hear if the fan is running?
faq.a: Open the freezer door and press the door switch. You should hear the evaporator fan running inside. If silence, the fan may be dead.

faq.q: Will E04 cause both compartments to warm up?
faq.a: Yes. The evaporator fan circulates cold air to both the freezer and fresh food compartments. If it fails, both sections will warm.

title: Defrost Heater Failure
shortDescription: The defrost heater is not operating during the defrost cycle.
fullDescription: Error E07 indicates the defrost heater failed to activate or did not produce enough heat during the defrost cycle. The control board monitors the defrost process by tracking evaporator temperature change. If the temperature does not rise as expected, the defrost heater is likely open or disconnected.

causes.cause: Defrost heater element has burned out
Defrost thermostat (bi-metal) is open
Wiring to the defrost heater is damaged
Main board defrost relay has failed

symptoms: Heavy frost on the evaporator coil
Ice buildup on the freezer back wall
Water puddles under the refrigerator from overflow

fixSteps.title: Manual defrost
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator and allow it to defrost completely to restore cooling temporarily.

fixSteps.title: Access the defrost heater
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the freezer back panel to expose the evaporator coil and defrost heater. The heater is a glass tube or wire loop beneath or around the evaporator.

fixSteps.title: Test the heater
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater and measure resistance. It should read 20-80 ohms. OL means the heater is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Replace the defrost heater
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old heater from its mounting clips or brackets around the evaporator. Install the new heater in the same position and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If both the heater and thermostat test fine, the main board defrost relay needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E07 defrost heater error fix

faq.q: How often does a Bosch refrigerator defrost?
faq.a: Most Bosch models defrost automatically every 8-12 hours, depending on compressor run time and usage patterns.

faq.q: Is the defrost thermostat part of the heater?
faq.a: No. The defrost thermostat is a separate safety device in series with the heater. It cuts power to the heater if the evaporator gets too warm.

faq.q: Can I run the refrigerator after a manual defrost?
faq.a: Yes, but E07 will return and frost will rebuild. Replace the defrost heater for a permanent fix.

title: Ice Maker Sensor or Motor Error
shortDescription: The ice maker system has detected a sensor or motor malfunction.
fullDescription: Error E08 indicates a fault in the ice maker system. This could be the ice maker thermostat, the ice maker motor that cycles the harvest mechanism, or the water inlet valve circuit. The ice maker will stop producing ice until the fault is resolved.

causes.cause: Ice maker thermostat or sensor has failed
Ice maker motor module has jammed or failed
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Ice maker wiring harness is damaged

symptoms: Ice maker makes a humming or clicking noise
Water fills but ice does not harvest

fixSteps.title: Reset the ice maker
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the ice maker reset button (usually on the side or bottom of the ice maker module). Press and hold for 10 seconds. Wait for it to cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for ice jams
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the ice bin and inspect the ice maker for any jammed ice cubes preventing the harvest arm or ejector blades from moving.

fixSteps.title: Test the ice maker module
fixSteps.instruction: Access the ice maker and check the motor for continuity. Also verify the thermostat reads the correct resistance.

fixSteps.title: Replace the ice maker
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor or thermostat has failed, the entire ice maker module is typically replaced as a unit. Disconnect wiring, remove mounting screws, and install the new module.

whenToCallPro: If the ice maker module and valve are fine, the control board ice maker circuit needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E08 ice maker error fix

faq.q: How long does it take to make the first batch of ice?
faq.a: After installation or reset, allow 24 hours for the freezer to reach optimal temperature and for the first batch of ice to form.

faq.q: Can I turn off the ice maker while waiting for repair?
faq.a: Yes. Most Bosch refrigerators have an ice maker on/off switch. Turning it off will stop the error and water fill attempts.

faq.q: Should I replace the whole ice maker or just the part?
faq.a: Bosch ice makers are typically sold as complete assemblies. Individual components like the thermostat or motor are usually not available separately.

title: Water Inlet Valve Error
shortDescription: The water inlet valve is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error E09 indicates the main control board has detected a fault with the water inlet valve. The valve may not be opening to fill the ice maker or water dispenser, or it may be stuck open, allowing continuous water flow. This error may also appear if water pressure is too low.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Water supply is turned off or has low pressure
Water filter is severely clogged, restricting flow
Control board valve driver circuit has failed

symptoms: Water dripping inside the refrigerator (stuck open valve)

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply shutoff valve behind or under the refrigerator is fully open. Check that the supply line is not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Replace the water filter
fixSteps.instruction: A severely clogged filter restricts flow and can trigger E09. Replace the filter and run 2-3 gallons through the dispenser to purge air.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator and access the water inlet valve (usually at the rear bottom). Test solenoid coil resistance, typically 200-500 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the water lines and electrical connector from the old valve. Remove mounting screws and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the valve and water supply are fine, the control board valve circuit needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E09 water inlet valve error fix

faq.q: What is the minimum water pressure for the valve?
faq.a: Most Bosch refrigerators require a minimum of 20 psi water pressure for the inlet valve to function properly.

faq.q: How often should I replace the water filter?
faq.a: Bosch recommends replacing the water filter every 6 months or when the filter indicator light turns on.

faq.q: Can a stuck-open valve cause water damage?
faq.a: Yes. A valve stuck in the open position will continuously fill, potentially causing water overflow and flooding. Unplug the refrigerator and turn off the water supply.

title: Compressor Overload or Start Failure
shortDescription: The compressor failed to start or has tripped its overload protection.
fullDescription: Error E11 indicates the compressor failed to start or tripped its thermal overload protector. The inverter board or relay cannot start the compressor motor, or the compressor has overheated due to excessive load, restricted airflow, or a failing compressor.

causes.cause: Compressor start relay or inverter board has failed
Compressor overload protector has tripped from overheating
Condenser coils are dirty, causing the compressor to overheat
Compressor motor is failing

symptoms: Clicking sound from the rear as the compressor tries to start
Compressor hums briefly then shuts off

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the refrigerator out and clean the condenser coils at the rear or bottom with a coil brush and vacuum. Dirty coils cause the compressor to overheat.

fixSteps.title: Let the compressor cool
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator for 30 minutes to allow the overload protector to reset. If the compressor starts after, monitor for recurrence.

fixSteps.title: Test the start relay or inverter
fixSteps.instruction: Access the compressor area. On conventional models, test the start relay for continuity. On inverter models, check the inverter board for visible damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace relay/inverter or compressor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the start relay (conventional) or inverter board (inverter compressor). If the compressor windings test bad, the compressor itself needs professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the compressor itself has failed, professional replacement with proper refrigerant handling is required.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E11 compressor start failure fix

faq.q: How do I know if the compressor is bad?
faq.a: Test compressor windings for proper resistance and check for shorts to ground. A compressor that fails these tests needs replacement by a certified technician.

faq.q: How often should condenser coils be cleaned?
faq.a: Every 6-12 months, or more often if you have pets. Pet hair is one of the biggest causes of condenser coil blockage.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the compressor?
faq.a: Compressor replacement is expensive, often $500-$1000 with labor. If the refrigerator is over 10 years old, replacement of the unit may be more cost-effective.

title: Communication Error Between Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and the display board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: Error E12 indicates a communication failure between the main control board (usually at the rear or bottom) and the user interface/display board (behind the front panel). These boards exchange data over a serial link and E12 means this link is broken.

causes.cause: Wire harness between boards is loose or damaged
Display board has failed
Main control board has failed
Power surge damaged communication circuits

symptoms: Display is blank or shows partial data
Refrigerator may still cool but settings cannot be changed

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and reconnect. This can reset the communication link.

fixSteps.title: Check wire harness
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top access panel. Locate the wire harness or ribbon cable connecting the main board to the display board. Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the harness for any pinched, cut, or corroded wires. Inspect both board connectors for burned pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If the harness is intact, one board has failed. A technician can test each to identify which needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connectors does not resolve E12, a professional can test both boards to identify the failure.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E12 communication error board fix

faq.q: Will the refrigerator still cool with E12?
faq.a: Sometimes. The main board may continue to run the compressor on its last known settings, but temperature cannot be adjusted.

faq.q: Which board is more likely to fail?
faq.a: Both fail at similar rates. A technician can test each board individually to determine which needs replacement.

faq.q: Can E12 be caused by a power outage?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges during an outage can damage the communication circuits on either board. A surge protector is recommended.

title: Water Leak Detected in Base Pan
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base pan area, triggering the leak protection system.
fullDescription: Error E15 is Bosch's water protection alarm. A float switch in the base pan area has been activated by accumulated water. This is a safety feature to prevent water damage. The refrigerator may shut down compressor operation until the water is removed and the cause is identified.

causes.cause: Defrost drain is clogged, water overflows into the base pan
Water supply line is leaking
Drain pan is cracked or overflowing
Float switch in the base is stuck or defective

symptoms: Refrigerator shuts down or reduces cooling
Water dripping from behind the refrigerator

fixSteps.title: Tilt the refrigerator back
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the refrigerator back about 15 degrees for 30 seconds to drain water away from the float switch. This may clear the immediate alarm.

fixSteps.title: Access and drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the kickplate at the bottom front. Locate the drain pan and remove accumulated water with towels or a wet-dry vacuum.

fixSteps.title: Clear the defrost drain
fixSteps.instruction: The defrost drain is the most common source of water in the base. Flush it with warm water from inside the freezer to clear ice or debris blockage.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the water supply line, inlet valve connections, and internal water lines for any drips or leaks.

whenToCallPro: If water continues to accumulate after clearing the drain and checking supply lines, call a professional to identify the leak source.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch refrigerator E15 water leak base pan error fix

faq.q: Is E15 an emergency?
faq.a: It is not immediately dangerous, but water accumulation can damage flooring and the refrigerator. Address it within a day.

faq.q: Why does the defrost drain clog?
faq.a: Food particles, ice, and mineral deposits can block the drain tube over time. It is the most common cause of E15 on Bosch refrigerators.

faq.q: Will tilting the refrigerator fix E15 permanently?
faq.a: No. Tilting only moves water off the float switch temporarily. You must find and fix the source of the water to prevent recurrence.
